devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । एवं निशम्य कपिलस्य वचो जनित्री । सा कर्दमस्य दयिता किल देवहूतिः । विस्रस्तमोहपटला तमभिप्रणम्य । तुष्टाव तत्त्वविषयाङ्कित ...
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca evaṃ niśamya kapilasya vaco janitrī sā kardamasya dayitā kila devahūtiḥ visrastamohapaṭalā tamabhipraṇamya tuṣṭāva tattvaviṣayāṅkita siddhibhūmim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
Having heard the discourse of Kapila, his mother Devahūti, the (beloved) wife of Kardama, got her veil of delusion torn open. She bowed to him and praised him who was the founder (lit. land) of the Sāṃkhya (system of philosophy) which is mainly characterised by the topic—treatment of Tattvas (principles).[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
tattva-viṣayāṅkita-siddha-bhūmim.
(i) Kapila was the patron of Yogins who are famous for their knowledge of the Tattvas viz. Prakṛti, Puruṣa and others—VṚ.
(ii) Kapila was the supporter of Brahmā and other numerous Śiddhas who are devoted to the Tattva, viz., Viṣṇu.—Padaratnāvalī
(iii) Kapila is so called because he was the past master in the Bhakti- tattva, Sāṃkhya-System, Yoga-tattva etc.—VC.
(iv) Kapila was the asylum of Siḍḍhis which are characterised by Vaiṣṇava-Sāṃkhya the main subject of which are the Tattvas, viz., cit. acit Brahman.—Siddhāntapradīpa
(v) Kapila was the preceptor of Siddhas who are characterised by the knowledge of Tattvas, viz., Prakṛti, Puruṣa, Īśvara etc.—Bālaprabodhini

devanagari: देवहूतिरुवाच । अथाप्यजोऽन्तःसलिले शयानं । भूतेन्द्रियार्थात्ममयं वपुस्ते । गुणप्रवाहं सदशेषबीजं । दध्यौ स्वयं यत् जठराब्जजातः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: devahūtiruvāca athāpyajo bhūtendriyārthātmamayaṃ vapuste guṇapravāhaṃ sadaśeṣabījaṃ dadhyau svayaṃ yat jaṭharābjajātaḥ

translation:
Devahūti said:
Even god Brahmā himself who was born of the lotus in your stomach (could not see your body but mentally) meditated upon your person which was lying manifest in the cosmic waters; which was the cause of the entire universe; which consisted of bhūtas (elements), sense-organs and their objects (e.g. fragrance, taste etc.), and the mind, and which has the flow (Sattva, Rajas, Tamas) in it.

devanagari: स एव विश्वस्य भवान् विधत्ते । गुणप्रवाहेण विभक्तवीर्यः । सर्गाद्यनीहोऽवितथाभिसन्धिः । आत्मेश्वरोऽतर्क्य सहस्रशक्तिः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva viśvasya bhavān vidhatte guṇapravāheṇa vibhaktavīryaḥ sargādyanīho ātmeśvaro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vidh class 6 verb]

translation: It is with your power divided by the flow of your guṇas that you (of above description), remaining inactive, bring about the creation, maintenance and destruction of the universe; Your will power is effective; You are the controller of all the jīvas (for whose enjoyment you create the universe; Your powers are infinite and beyond comprehension.

devanagari: स त्वं भृतो मे जठरेण नाथ । कथं नु यस्योदर एतदासीत् । विश्वं युगान्ते वटपत्र एकः । शेते स्म मायाशिशुरङ्घ्रिपानः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tvaṃ bhṛto me jaṭhareṇa nātha kathaṃ nu yasyodara etadāsīt viśvaṃ yugānte vaṭapatra ekaḥ śete sma māyāśiśuraṅghripānaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: Oh Lord, how was it possible that you were borne by me in my womb. It was your Māyā, you in whose stomach lay the whole of the universe, and who, at the end of Yugas (i.e. after the deluge set in) lay alone on a banyan leaf in the form of an infant sucking its toe.

devanagari: त्वं देहतन्त्रः प्रशमाय पाप्मनां । निदेशभाजां च विभो विभूतये । यथावतारास्तव सूकरादयः । तथायमप्यात्म पथोपलब्धये ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ dehatantraḥ praśamāya pāpmanāṃ nideśabhājāṃ ca vibho vibhūtaye yathāvatārāstava sūkarādayaḥ tathāyamapyātma pathopalabdhaye
translation: Oh Supreme Lord, you have assumed the corporeal form for the destruction of the wicked sinners and for the prosperity of those who obey your commands. Just as you have your (other) incarnations of Boar etc., this incarnation (of yours) is for showing the path of self-realization.

devanagari: यन्नामधेयश्रवणानुकीर्तनाद् । यत्प्रह्वणाद् यत् स्मरणादपि क्वचित् । श्वादोऽपि सद्यः सवनाय कल्पते । कुतः पुनस्ते भगवन्नु दर्शनात् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: yannāmadheyaśravaṇānukīrtanād yatprahvaṇād yat smaraṇādapi kvacit śvādo kutaḥ punaste bhagavannu darśanāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Oh glorious Lord, even a Cāṇḍāla (lit. a dog-eater) immediately becomes worthy (like a performer) of the Soma Sacrifice, if he has but once heard or uttered your name or bowed to you or remembered you. What need be said of a person (like me) who has (directly) seen you?

devanagari: अहो बत श्वपचोऽतो गरीयान् । यज्जिह्वाग्रे वर्तते नाम तुभ्यम् । तेपुस्तपस्ते जुहुवुः सस्नुरार्या । ब्रह्मानूचुर्नाम गृणन्ति ये ते ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: aho bata śvapaco yajjihvāgre vartate nāma tubhyam tepustapaste juhuvuḥ sasnurāryā brahmānūcurnāma gṛṇanti ye te
translation: Oh how wonderful it is that even a Cāṇḍāla (the lowest-born person) becomes superior and worthy of respect simply because Your name is on the tip of his tongue. Those persons of noble behaviour who take your name have (the merit of having) performed penance, sacrifices and baths in holy waters, and Vedic studies (Or: It is as a result of doing these meritorious acts in the previous life that they take your name in this birth).

devanagari: तं त्वामहं ब्रह्म परं पुमांसं । प्रत्यक्स्रोतस्यात्मनि संविभाव्यम् । स्वतेजसा ध्वस्तगुणप्रवाहं । वन्दे विष्णुं कपिलं वेदगर्भम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tvāmahaṃ brahma paraṃ pumāṃsaṃ pratyaksrotasyātmani saṃvibhāvyam svatejasā dhvastaguṇapravāhaṃ vande viṣṇuṃ kapilaṃ vedagarbham
translation: I pay obeisance to you, Kapila, who are the highest Brahman, the Supreme Man who are worthy of meditation in mind which is withdrawn from external objects, and who have dried up the flow of guṇas by your brilliance and who are Viṣṇu (who holds Vedas within him).

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । ईडितो भगवानेवं कपिलाख्यः परः पुमान् । वाचाविक्लवयेत्याह मातरं मातृवत्सलः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca īḍito bhagavānevaṃ kapilākhyaḥ paraḥ pumān vācāviklavayetyāha mātaraṃ mātṛvatsalaḥ

translation:
Maitreya said:
The Supreme Man called the venerable Kapila who was thus praised and who was affectionate to his mother, spoke thus to her in words deep insignificance (Or: with words choked up with emotions.).

devanagari: कपिल उवाच । मार्गेणानेन मातस्ते सुसेव्येनोदितेन मे । आस्थितेन परां काष्ठां अचिराद् अवरोत्स्यसि ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: kapila uvāca mārgeṇānena mātaste susevyenoditena me āsthitena parāṃ kāṣṭhāṃ acirād avarotsyasi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Kapila said:
Oh mother, I have explained to you the path which is easy to follow. By following this path, you will reach the highest stage (of being liberated while alive).

devanagari: श्रद्धत्स्वैतन्मतं मह्यं जुष्टं यद्‍ब्रह्मवादिभिः । येन मां अभयं याया मृत्युमृच्छन्त्यतद्विदः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śraddhatsvaitanmataṃ mahyaṃ juṣṭaṃ yad‍brahmavādibhiḥ yena māṃ abhayaṃ yāyā mṛtyumṛcchantyatadvidaḥ

translation: Have faith in my doctrine which has been followed by the knowers of Brahman (or the Vedas). By following this you will attain to me who am without birth (i.e. eternal). Those who are ignorant about this path go to death (saṃsāra).

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इति प्रदर्श्य भगवान् सतीं तां आत्मनो गतिम् । स्वमात्रा ब्रह्मवादिन्या कपिलोऽनुमतो ययौ ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca iti pradarśya bhagavān satīṃ tāṃ ātmano gatim svamātrā brahmavādinyā kapilo

translation:
Maitreya said:
The venerable Kapila showed to his pious mother the path leading to Ātman. Having obtained the permission of his mother, who realized the Brahman, Kapila departed (from her hermitage).

devanagari: सा चापि तनयोक्तेन योगादेशेन योगयुक् । तस्मिन् आश्रम आपीडे सरस्वत्याः समाहिता ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sā cāpi tanayoktena yogādeśena yogayuk tasmin āśrama āpīḍe sarasvatyāḥ samāhitā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ vac class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: And in that hermitage which was like a flowerchaplet on the head of the Sarasvatī (river), she adopted Yogic practice in accordance with the guidance of her son, and became composed in mind.

devanagari: अभीक्ष्ण अवगाहकपिशान् जटिलान् कुटिलालकान् । आत्मानं च उग्रतपसा बिभ्रती चीरिणं कृशम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: abhīkṣṇa avagāhakapiśān jaṭilān kuṭilālakān ātmānaṃ ca ugratapasā bibhratī cīriṇaṃ kṛśam
translation: She performed ablutions three times a day. Her curly hair became matted and tawny in colour. By severe penance her body became emaciated. She wore bark-garments.

devanagari: प्रजापतेः कर्दमस्य तपोयोगविजृम्भितम् । स्वगार्हस्थ्यमनौपम्यं प्रार्थ्यं वैमानिकैरपि ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: prajāpateḥ kardamasya tapoyogavijṛmbhitam svagārhasthyamanaupamyaṃ prārthyaṃ vaimānikairapi
translation: The household comforts that were created by Prajāpati Kardama by means of his penance and Yoga were incomparable. They were covetable even to gods.

devanagari: पयःफेननिभाः शय्या दान्ता रुक्मपरिच्छदाः । आसनानि च हैमानि सुस्पर्शास्तरणानि च ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: payaḥphenanibhāḥ śayyā dāntā rukmaparicchadāḥ āsanāni ca haimāni susparśāstaraṇāni ca
translation: The beds were white and soft like the foam of milk. The ivory couches were chased with gold. The seats of gold were provided with soft cushions (covers).

devanagari: स्वच्छस्फटिककुड्येषु महामारकतेषु च । रत्‍नप्रदीपा आभान्ति ललना रत्‍नसंयुताः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: svacchasphaṭikakuḍyeṣu mahāmārakateṣu ca rat‍napradīpā ābhānti lalanā rat‍nasaṃyutāḥ
translation: In the walls of transparent crystals and very costly emeralds were shining jewel-lamps along with statues of beautiful damsels made of precious stones.

devanagari: गृहोद्यानं कुसुमितै रम्यं बह्वमरद्रुमैः । कूजद् विहङ्गमिथुनं गायन् मत्तमधुव्रतम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhodyānaṃ kusumitai ramyaṃ bahvamaradrumaiḥ kūjad vihaṅgamithunaṃ gāyan mattamadhuvratam

translation: The garden around the house looked beautiful with many blossoming celestial trees, on which couples of birds were warbling and the intoxicated black bees were humming sweetly.

devanagari: यत्र प्रविष्टमात्मानं विबुधानुचरा जगुः । वाप्यां उत्पलगन्धिन्यां कर्दमेनोपलालितम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra praviṣṭamātmānaṃ vibudhānucarā jaguḥ vāpyāṃ utpalagandhinyāṃ kardamenopalālitam
translation: Where the followers of gods (Gandharvas) used to praise her when she, fondled by Kardama, entered in the swimming pool fragrant with lotuses.

devanagari: हित्वा तदीप्सिततमं अप्याखण्डलयोषिताम् । किञ्चिच्चकार वदनं पुत्रविश्लेषणातुरा ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: hitvā tadīpsitatamaṃ apyākhaṇḍalayoṣitām kiñciccakāra vadanaṃ putraviśleṣaṇāturā

translation: It (the household) was most covetable even to the queens of Indra. But she just abandoned it. Due to the anxiety caused by the separation of her son, she was dejected in appearance.

devanagari: वनं प्रव्रजिते पत्यौ अपत्यविरहातुरा । ज्ञाततत्त्वाप्यभून्नष्टे वत्से गौरिव वत्सला ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: vanaṃ pravrajite patyau apatyavirahāturā jñātatattvāpyabhūnnaṣṭe vatse gauriva vatsalā

translation: As her husband left for the forest (after renouncing the world) Devahūti, though she realized the Truth (the Sāṃkhya Principles), became overanxious due to the separation from her son, like a calf-loving cow is fond after her young one.

devanagari: तमेव ध्यायती देवं अपत्यं कपिलं हरिम् । बभूवाचिरतो वत्स निःस्पृहा तादृशे गृहे ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tameva dhyāyatī devaṃ apatyaṃ kapilaṃ harim babhūvācirato vatsa niḥspṛhā tādṛśe gṛhe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Oh child (Vidura), she meditated upon her child Kapila who was the God Hari. In a short time, she became indifferent to that type of rich household.

devanagari: ध्यायती भगवद्रूपं यदाह ध्यानगोचरम् । सुतः प्रसन्नवदनं समस्तव्यस्तचिन्तया ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: dhyāyatī bhagavadrūpaṃ yadāha dhyānagocaram sutaḥ prasannavadanaṃ samastavyastacintayā
translation: According to the guide-lines given by her son, she meditated upon the form of the gracious-looking Lord—the object of meditation—by the method of contemplating the complete form followed by concentration on the parts of it.[2]

devanagari: भक्तिप्रवाहयोगेन वैराग्येण बलीयसा । युक्तानुष्ठानजातेन ज्ञानेन ब्रह्महेतुना ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: bhaktipravāhayogena vairāgyeṇa balīyasā yuktānuṣṭhānajātena jñānena brahmahetunā
translation: By means of stream or overflow of devotion, by powerful renunciation, and by knowledge which was produced by the proper observances of vows[3] leading to Brahma-hood.

devanagari: विशुद्धेन तदात्मानं आत्मना विश्वतोमुखम् । स्वानुभूत्या तिरोभूत मायागुणविशेषणम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: viśuddhena tadātmānaṃ ātmanā viśvatomukham svānubhūtyā tirobhūta māyāguṇaviśeṣaṇam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Her mind became pure. She was then lost in the meditation of all-pervading Atman who by his essential light removes the limitations of the guṇas of Māyā.

devanagari: ब्रह्मण्यवस्थितमतिः भगवति आत्मसंश्रये । निवृत्तजीवापत्तित्वात् क्षीणक्लेशाऽप्त निर्वृतिः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmaṇyavasthitamatiḥ bhagavati ātmasaṃśraye nivṛttajīvāpattitvāt kṣīṇakleśā
translation: Her mind became steadied in the glorious Brahman which is the shelter of all the jīvas. As she superseded her state of being a jīva, all her afflictions were over and she attained to the blessed stage.

devanagari: नित्यारूढसमाधित्वात् परावृत्तगुणभ्रमा । न सस्मार तदाऽत्मानं स्वप्ने दृष्टमिवोत्थितः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: nityārūḍhasamādhitvāt parāvṛttaguṇabhramā na sasmāra tadā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb]

translation: As she was always engaged in continuous meditation and her delusion due to guṇas was dispelled, at that time she did not remember her body, just as an object seen in a dream (is found to be unreal) after waking up.

devanagari: तद्देहः परतः पोषोऽपि अकृशश्चाध्यसम्भवात् । बभौ मलैरवच्छन्नः सधूम इव पावकः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: taddehaḥ parataḥ poṣo babhau malairavacchannaḥ sadhūma iva pāvakaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Her body was fed by others (viz. Vidyādhara damsels who were produced by Kardama for attendance upon her). As she was free from diseases, she did not become emaciated. Her body was covered with filth. She looked like a fire covered with smoke.

devanagari: स्वाङ्गं तपोयोगमयं मुक्तकेशं गताम्बरम् । दैवगुप्तं न बुबुधे वासुदेवप्रविष्टधीः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: svāṅgaṃ tapoyogamayaṃ muktakeśaṃ gatāmbaram daivaguptaṃ na bubudhe vāsudevapraviṣṭadhīḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ gup class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ gup class 4 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Her mind was so deeply entered (i.e. absorbed) in Vāsudeva that she was not conscious of her body. She had so completely given herself up to penance and Yoga that her hair fell dishevelled and clothes were dropped aṇd she was protected by her previous karma (daiva).

devanagari: एवं सा कपिलोक्तेन मार्गेणाचिरतः परम् । आत्मानं ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं भगवन्तं अवाप ह ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sā kapiloktena mārgeṇācirataḥ param ātmānaṃ brahmanirvāṇaṃ bhagavantaṃ avāpa ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ vac class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[instrumental single from √ vac class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: In this way she progressed by the path expounded by Kapila. Within a short period she attained to the Supreme Soul, the Brahman, the glorious Lord; the stage called Nirvāṇa.

devanagari: तद्वीरासीत् पुण्यतमं क्षेत्रं त्रैलोक्यविश्रुतम् । नाम्ना सिद्धपदं यत्र सा संसिद्धिमुपेयुषी ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tadvīrāsīt puṇyatamaṃ kṣetraṃ trailokyaviśrutam nāmnā siddhapadaṃ yatra sā saṃsiddhimupeyuṣī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: Oh warrior (Vidura), the place called Siddhapada where she attained the Liberation became famous as the holiest place in the three worlds.

devanagari: तस्यास्तद्योगविधुतमार्त्यं मर्त्यमभूत्सरित् । स्रोतसां प्रवरा सौम्य सिद्धिदा सिद्धसेविता ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāstadyogavidhutamārtyaṃ martyamabhūtsarit srotasāṃ pravarā saumya siddhidā siddhasevitā
translation: Oh gentle Vidura, her mortal body from which impurities were eliminated by Yoga, was transformed into a river, a prominent one among many rivers. It blesses one with siddhis and is resorted to by Siddhas.

devanagari: कपिलोऽपि महायोगी भगवान् पितुराश्रमात् । मातरं समनुज्ञाप्य प्राग् उदीचीं दिशं ययौ ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: kapilo mātaraṃ samanujñāpya prāg udīcīṃ diśaṃ yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Even glorious Kapila, the great Yogin, obtained the permission of his mother to depart from his father’s hermitage and proceeded to the northern direction.

devanagari: सिद्धचारण गन्धर्वैः मुनिभिश्च अप्सरोगणैः । स्तूयमानः समुद्रेण दत्तार्हणनिकेतनः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: siddhacāraṇa gandharvaiḥ munibhiśca apsarogaṇaiḥ stūyamānaḥ samudreṇa dattārhaṇaniketanaḥ
translation: He was praised by multitude of Siddhas, Cāraṇas, Gandharvas, sages and celestial nymphs. He was also respectfully received by the sea and was presented a valuable dwelling place.

devanagari: आस्ते योगं समास्थाय साङ्ख्याचार्यैरभिष्टुतः । त्रयाणामपि लोकानां उपशान्त्यै समाहितः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: āste yogaṃ samāsthāya sāṅkhyācāryairabhiṣṭutaḥ trayāṇāmapi lokānāṃ upaśāntyai samāhitaḥ
translation: Kapila stays there practising Yoga. He is highly eulogized by the great teachers of Sāṃkhya School. For the peace and tranquillity of the three worlds, he lives there (absorbed) in meditation.

devanagari: एतन्निगदितं तात यत्पृष्टोऽहं तवानघ । कपिलस्य च संवादो देवहूत्याश्च पावनः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: etannigaditaṃ tāta yatpṛṣṭo kapilasya ca saṃvādo devahūtyāśca pāvanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Oh child (Vidura), as per your query I have narrated to you the sacred dialogue between Kapila and Deva- hūti, Oh sinless one.

devanagari: य इदमनुशृणोति योऽभिधत्ते । कपिलमुनेर्मतं आत्मयोगगुह्यम् । भगवति कृतधीः सुपर्णकेतौ । उपलभते भगवत् पदारविन्दम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: ya idamanuśṛṇoti yo kapilamunermataṃ ātmayogaguhyam bhagavati kṛtadhīḥ suparṇaketau upalabhate bhagavat padāravindam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ guh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ guh class 1 verb]

translation:
He who listens to this or narrates this doctrine of the Sage Kapila regarding the secret knowledge about the Ātman, becomes able to concentrate his mind upon the venerable Lord whose banner has the emblem of Garuḍa, and he attains to the lotus-like feet of the glorious Lord.
